back_translation: “I have told these things to you, so that you will not stumble and go astray.
They will drive you out of the synagogues, and that time is coming when everyone who kills you will think that he is offering service to God.
And they will do these things because they do not know the Father or Me.

But I have told these things to you, so that when the time of these things comes, you will remember that I told you in advance. I did not tell you these things from the very beginning, because I was still with you.
But now I am going to the One who sent Me, and none of you asks Me, ‘Where will You go?’
But because I have told you these things, sorrow has filled your heart.
But I tell you the truth — it is to your benefit that I am going away. For if I do not go away, the Helper will not come to you. But if I go, I will send Him to you.
And when He comes, He will rebuke the world concerning sin, righteousness, and judgment.
Concerning sin, because they do not believe in Me.
Concerning righteousness, because I am going to the Father, and you will no longer see Me.
And concerning judgment, because the ruler of this world has already been judged.
I still have many things to tell you, but right now you cannot bear to receive them.

But when the Spirit of Truth comes, He will guide you into all truth. For He will not speak from His own authority, but He will speak according to what He hears, and He will declare to you the things that are about to come.
He will cause Me to receive glory, because He will take from what is Mine and declare it to you.
Everything that the Father has is Mine. Therefore I said that the Spirit will take from what is Mine and declare it to you.
In a little while you will not see Me, and in a little while you will see Me.

So some of His disciples asked one another, “What does He mean when He tells us, ‘In a little while you will not see Me, and in a little while you will see Me,’ and ‘Because I will go to the Father’?”
Then they kept saying, “What does He mean by ‘in a little while’? We do not understand what He is saying.”
Jesus knew that they wanted to ask Him, so He said to them, “Are you asking one another about what I said, ‘In a little while you will not see Me, and in a little while you will see Me’?
Truly, truly, I say to you, you will weep and lament; but the world will rejoice. You will grieve, but your grief will turn into joy.

When a woman is about to give birth, she has pain, because her time has come. But when she has given birth, she no longer remembers that suffering, because of the joy that someone has been born into the world.
You also — now you have grief, but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will be able to take your joy away from you.
And in that day you will no longer ask Me anything. Truly, truly, I say to you, whatever you ask the Father in My name, He will grant to you.
Until now you have not asked anything in My name. Ask, and you will receive, so that your joy may be made full.
I have told these things to you in figures of speech. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures of speech, but I will tell you about the Father openly.
In that day you will ask in My name, and I do not say to you that I will petition the Father for you.
For the Father Himself loves you, because you have loved Me and have believed that I came from God.
I came out from the Father and entered the world. Now I will leave the world and go to the Father again.
His disciples said, “See, now You are speaking openly, and not using any figures of speech.
Now we know that You know all things, and there is no need for anyone to ask You. Because of this, we believe that You came from God.”
Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
Behold, the time is coming, and has already arrived, when you will be scattered, each going to his own home, and you will leave Me alone. But I am not alone, because the Father is with Me.
I have told these things to you so that you may have peace in Me. In the world you will have tribulation; but take courage, I have overcome the world!”
